title: dorstenia oligogyna
intro: Dorstenia oligogyna is a species of flowering plant in the family Moraceae, commonly known as the forest jewel or West African jewel. It has been traditionally used in West African folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Dorstenia oligogyna include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, making it a sought-after remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Dorstenia oligogyna are typically made by boiling the leaves in water or using them in combination with other herbs to create infusions or decoctions, although its availability and usage may vary depending on the region.
